members.claim: WHM yielded distinct patterns compared to baseline, characterized by scalp frontopolar delta, left-posterior low-beta, strong posterior gamma, source left superior temporal delta, left lateral occipital alpha suppression, right precuneus beta, left precuneus/right paracentral highgamma, connectivity theta in sensorimotor-salience hubs and gamma linking posterior DMN to ACC/lateral PFC, and physiology deeper hypocapnia (~2.54% EtCO₂), steadier RR (~10.1/min), and higher pulse (~76.4 bpm).

members.claim: The practice is a deliberate method to shift positive bodily states through cyclic breathing paired with graded cold exposure, targeting autonomic, interoceptive awareness, and immune control.

members.claim: Each round reproduced the canonical three sub-phases: approximately 30-40 paced power breaths with relaxed exhalations, a low-lung-volume breath-hold without a body squeeze, and a single full-inhalation recovery breath held for 10-15 seconds before release.

members.claim: The protocol consisted of three consecutive rounds lasting approximately 3-3.5 minutes each, totaling about 10-12 minutes, followed by a quiet post-breath rest.

members.claim: Cutaneous temperature was logged at three trunk sites chosen a priori to differentiate localised thermogenesis from generalised vasomotor effects: (i) the supraclavicular fossa, overlying a principal human brown-adipose-tissue (BAT) depot, to probe BAT-linked heat production; (ii) the mid-sternal/upper chest as a core-proximal trunk reference that is less BAT-dense; and (iii) the upper thoracic paraspinal region (approx. T1 -T4) to index trunk skin under typical clothing/insulation and posture-related convection.
members.doc_name: 6- Hypocapnia Autonomic Signatures and Gamma-Band Reconfiguration During Wim Hof Breathing Method and Cold Immersion

members.claim: Beta rhythms (~13 -30 Hz) increase when the brain maintains an ongoing sensorimotor or cognitive 'set' (the 'status quo' hypothesis), which supports the maintenance of the current sensorimotor or cognitive state and strengthens top-down control across distributed networks.

members.claim: In motor/respiratory contexts, beta rhythms support sustained control and predictive timing loops between motor/pre-motor regions and sensory areas, which is exactly what paced, guided breathing demands.

members.rationale: The text explicitly states that beta rhythms support sustained control and predictive timing loops in motor/respiratory contexts, noting this is exactly what paced, guided breathing demands.

members.claim: A condition that raises ventilation, typically producing hypocapnia (i.e., a state of low carbon dioxide levels in the blood, below 35 mmHg)/ and respiratory alkalosis (hypocapnia but also causing blood pH to rise above normal), and a transient surge in sympathetic nervous system activity surge, triggering heightened alertness, increased heart rate (HR), and other fight-or-flight responses.
members.doc_name: 6- Hypocapnia Autonomic Signatures and Gamma-Band Reconfiguration During Wim Hof Breathing Method and Cold Immersion

members.claim: neural signatures in trained versus naïve practitioners remain incompletely characterised

members.claim: Hyperventilation-breath-retention cycles and their associated blood-gas perturbations, combined with cold exposure, are posited to recruit mechanisms spanning sympathetic outflow, anti-inflammatory signalling, and top-down regulation of internal sensation.
members.doc_name: 6- Hypocapnia Autonomic Signatures and Gamma-Band Reconfiguration During Wim Hof Breathing Method and Cold Immersion
members.rationale: The Introduction explicitly states that WHM components (hyperventilation-breath-retention cycles and cold exposure) are posited to recruit mechanisms spanning sympathetic outflow, anti-inflammatory signalling, and top-down regulation of internal sensation.

members.claim: neural endpoints regarding the physiological effects of Whole Body Hyperthermia (WHM) remain sparse, with no systematic characterization of WHM neurophysiology or concurrent multimodal recordings capturing EEG alongside autonomic and respiratory measures during a standardised WHM breathing bout.
members.doc_name: 6- Hypocapnia Autonomic Signatures and Gamma-Band Reconfiguration During Wim Hof Breathing Method and Cold Immersion
members.rationale: The text defines WHM as a cold exposure practice, whereas the claim incorrectly identifies it as Whole Body Hyperthermia. Additionally, the text explicitly states the study fills the gap regarding sparse neural endpoints, contradicting the claim's assertion that no such characterization exists.

members.claim: Although many studies have shown that respiratory rhythms influence a variety of behavioural effects in cognitive, emotional, and perceptual areas, a complete framework to clarify these effects remains to be fully developed.

members.rationale: The Introduction explicitly states that while interest in WHM's physiological effects is growing, neural endpoints remain sparse and concurrent multimodal recordings are uncommon. This directly supports the claim that a complete framework remains to be fully developed.

members.claim: The study examines healthy participants under a standardized guided protocol, stratified by experience (naïve vs Level 2 WHM practitioners), to test whether canonical sympathetic activation patterns extend to healthy novices and whether expertise consolidates responses into reproducible neural signatures.

members.claim: Considering that hyperventilation-induced hypocapnia reduces CBF, classically evoking a slow-wave build-up Delta that is generally frontally dominant and is mechanistically associated with vasoconstriction and reduced perfusion, our predictions suggest that we will see enhanced Delta activity during WHM.
members.doc_name: 6- Hypocapnia Autonomic Signatures and Gamma-Band Reconfiguration During Wim Hof Breathing Method and Cold Immersion
members.section_heading: A Priori Hypotheses and Multimodal Predictions
members.rationale: The text explicitly states this prediction in the 'A Priori Hypotheses' section, linking hyperventilation-induced hypocapnia to reduced CBF and frontally dominant Delta activity.

members.claim: Hypothetically, we should observe lower alpha in the sensorimotor cortex and the salience/insula, as these circuits are responsible for processing respiratory effort and internal signals.
members.doc_name: 6- Hypocapnia Autonomic Signatures and Gamma-Band Reconfiguration During Wim Hof Breathing Method and Cold Immersion
members.section_heading: A Priori Hypotheses and Multimodal Predictions
members.rationale: The claim is explicitly stated as a prediction in the 'A Priori Hypotheses' section, directly supported by the text's reasoning regarding respiratory effort and interoception.

members.claim: we hypothesised that WHM would increase phasic EDA (SCR frequency, amplitude, area) during the breathing bouts -indexing transient sympathetic bursts that co-occur with the epinephrine surge.

members.section_heading: A Priori Hypotheses and Multimodal Predictions

members.claim: In WHM, sudomotor nerve bursting would provide a quantitative proxy for the central arousal dynamics of the LC -NE, which should be more phasic during WHM and express more tonic levels in baseline and rest phases.

members.section_heading: A Priori Hypotheses and Multimodal Predictions

members.section_heading: A Priori Hypotheses and Multimodal Predictions

members.claim: If deliberate hyperventilation/retention cycles recruit the preBötC and LC pathways to up-modulate arousal, this should be expressed peripherally as increased skin conductance responses (SCRs) frequency and larger SCR amplitude/area during the breathing phase, with partial normalisation at rest.
members.doc_name: 6- Hypocapnia Autonomic Signatures and Gamma-Band Reconfiguration During Wim Hof Breathing Method and Cold Immersion
members.section_heading: A Priori Hypotheses and Multimodal Predictions
members.rationale: The text explicitly states this hypothesis in the 'A Priori Hypotheses' section. The 'Capnography Results' section confirms the expected physiological shift, noting increased SCR frequency, amplitude, and area during the breathing phase with normalization at rest, matching the predicted outcome.

members.claim: The study aims to test whether brown adipose tissue (BAT) thermogenesis is triggered during whole-body hypometabolism (WHM) and whether this reflects sympathetic nervous system activation, based on prior temperature changes reported in similar breathing techniques.
members.doc_name: 6- Hypocapnia Autonomic Signatures and Gamma-Band Reconfiguration During Wim Hof Breathing Method and Cold Immersion
members.section_heading: A Priori Hypotheses and Multimodal Predictions
members.rationale: The text explicitly states the decision to conduct exploratory research to test if BAT thermogenesis is triggered during WHM and if it reflects sympathetic activation, citing prior temperature changes in similar breathing techniques.

members.claim: Prior research by Muzik et al. indicated that cold-activated brown adipose tissue (BAT) was small and metabolically unremarkable, whereas glucose uptake (FDG SUV) and sympathetic innervation (HED retention index) were selectively elevated in intercostal muscles during cold exposure, implicating respiratory work as a principal heat source that warms pulmonary blood.
members.doc_name: 6- Hypocapnia Autonomic Signatures and Gamma-Band Reconfiguration During Wim Hof Breathing Method and Cold Immersion
members.section_heading: A Priori Hypotheses and Multimodal Predictions
members.rationale: The claim is directly supported by the text in the '[A Priori Hypotheses and Multimodal Predictions]' section, which explicitly cites Muzik et al. and details their findings regarding BAT, glucose uptake, and sympathetic innervation in intercostal muscles.

members.claim: Gamma bursts at P1/SPL during Whole-Body Movement (WHM) plausibly represent phase-locked, precision-weighted proprioceptive/interoceptive updates riding on top of slower feedback control.
members.doc_name: 6- Hypocapnia Autonomic Signatures and Gamma-Band Reconfiguration During Wim Hof Breathing Method and Cold Immersion
members.rationale: The text explicitly states that gamma bursts at P1/SPL during WHM plausibly represent phase-locked, precision-weighted proprioceptive/interoceptive updates riding on top of slower feedback control, directly matching the claim.

members.claim: Noradrenergic and cholinergic arousal robustly engaged by cold stress is likely to reduce theta synchrony and explain the shift towards higher-frequency oscillations, such as Gamma, as a function of Norepinephrine ignition.
members.doc_name: 6- Hypocapnia Autonomic Signatures and Gamma-Band Reconfiguration During Wim Hof Breathing Method and Cold Immersion
members.rationale: The text explicitly states that noradrenergic and cholinergic arousal from cold stress likely reduces theta synchrony and explains the shift to higher-frequency oscillations like Gamma due to Norepinephrine ignition.

members.claim: The study addresses the knowledge gap regarding the specific frequency signatures and neural mechanisms underlying the perception of cold water when visual input is absent.
members.doc_name: 6- Hypocapnia Autonomic Signatures and Gamma-Band Reconfiguration During Wim Hof Breathing Method and Cold Immersion
members.rationale: The text explicitly links mid-Gamma FC activity in the lateral occipital cortex to tactile discrimination and haptic sensations, specifically noting this occurs when vision is unavailable during cold water immersion. The Results section supports this by detailing specific posterior brain regions (e.g., left lateral occipital, right precuneus) showing distinct patterns in experts compared to novices.

members.claim: Compared with baseline, the breathing session increased beta and gamma spectral power and produced frequency-specific changes in functional connectivity, most prominently within theta and gamma bands.
members.doc_name: 6- Hypocapnia Autonomic Signatures and Gamma-Band Reconfiguration During Wim Hof Breathing Method and Cold Immersion
members.rationale: The Key Messages section explicitly states that the breathing session increased beta and gamma spectral power and produced frequency-specific changes in functional connectivity, most prominently within theta and gamma bands, directly matching the claim.

members.claim: Expertise modulated network organization during the breathing session, with Level 2 practitioners showing theta connectivity emphasizing sensorimotor and salience hubs and gamma coupling linking posterior default mode regions with anterior cingulate and lateral prefrontal cortices, whereas novices showed stronger default mode to visual coupling and more posteriorly weighted gamma connectivity.
members.doc_name: 6- Hypocapnia Autonomic Signatures and Gamma-Band Reconfiguration During Wim Hof Breathing Method and Cold Immersion
members.rationale: The Key Messages section explicitly states that expertise modulated network organization, detailing specific connectivity differences between Level 2 practitioners and novices regarding theta and gamma bands, directly supporting the claim.

members.claim: In Level 2 practitioners, cold water immersion further amplified alpha and gamma connectivity, enhancing posterior to frontal coupling and somatosensory to visual integration.
members.doc_name: 6- Hypocapnia Autonomic Signatures and Gamma-Band Reconfiguration During Wim Hof Breathing Method and Cold Immersion
members.rationale: The Key Messages section explicitly states that in Level 2 practitioners, cold water immersion amplified alpha and gamma connectivity, enhancing posterior to frontal coupling and somatosensory to visual integration, directly matching the claim.

members.claim: Hyperventilation-breath-retention cycles and their associated blood-gas perturbations, combined with cold exposure, are posited to recruit mechanisms spanning sympathetic outflow, anti-inflammatory signalling, and top-down regulation of internal sensation.
members.doc_name: 6- Hypocapnia Autonomic Signatures and Gamma-Band Reconfiguration During Wim Hof Breathing Method and Cold Immersion
members.rationale: The Introduction explicitly states that WHM components (hyperventilation-breath-retention cycles and cold exposure) are posited to recruit mechanisms spanning sympathetic outflow, anti-inflammatory signalling, and top-down regulation of internal sensation.

members.claim: the present study aims to quantify WHM breathing's acute neurophysiological and autonomic profile using simultaneous EEG and peripheral physiology, and to test whether training level shapes these responses by comparing naïve participants with Level-2 practitioners.
members.doc_name: 6- Hypocapnia Autonomic Signatures and Gamma-Band Reconfiguration During Wim Hof Breathing Method and Cold Immersion
members.rationale: The Introduction explicitly states the study's aim to quantify WHM's acute neurophysiological and autonomic profile using simultaneous EEG and peripheral physiology, and to test if training level shapes responses by comparing naïve participants with Level-2 practitioners.

members.claim: Findings link respiration to interoceptive and attentional control, highlighting the involvement of higher brain circuits beyond brainstem reflexes.

members.claim: Nasal respiration entrains delta/theta power in piriform cortex, amygdala, and hippocampus, strengthening theta-beta cross-frequency coupling in piriform cortex to improve behavior (faster fear discrimination; better recognition memory) specifically during inhalation.

members.claim: Effects of nasal respiration on brain oscillations largely vanish with mouth breathing, underscoring a nasal/olfactory pathway for respiration-brain coupling.

members.rationale: The text explicitly states that effects of nasal respiration on brain oscillations vanish with mouth breathing, highlighting a nasal/olfactory pathway.

members.claim: Airflow-driven rhythms and brainstem pattern generators propagate to locus coeruleus, modulating neural gain and cross-frequency coupling to facilitate predictive coding across sensory, interoceptive, and cognitive systems.

members.claim: Considering that hyperventilation-induced hypocapnia reduces CBF, classically evoking a slow-wave build-up Delta that is generally frontally dominant and is mechanistically associated with vasoconstriction and reduced perfusion, our predictions suggest that we will see enhanced Delta activity during WHM.

members.section_heading: A Priori Hypotheses and Multimodal Predictions

members.claim: Hypothetically, we should observe lower alpha in the sensorimotor cortex and the salience/insula, as these circuits are responsible for processing respiratory effort and internal signals.

members.section_heading: A Priori Hypotheses and Multimodal Predictions

members.claim: WHM should also produce intense interoceptive focus and respiratory effort, which can increase gamma activity, consistent with fast-band integration of somatosensory, salience/insula, and control hubs.

members.claim: This exploration aligns with Locus-Coeruleus-Norepinephrine (LC-NE) function and classical theories of the autonomous nervous system (ANS), which suggest that phasic and tonic modes of LC activity are associated with different arousal and behavioural states, correlating with changes in EDA as a measure of autonomic engagement.

members.claim: Applied to our WHM protocol, all three metrics increased from Baseline to WHM and then declined during the post-breathing Rest period; this phase-dependent pattern was evident in both naïve and Level-2 practitioners with no reliable between-group differences, although the WHM versus Rest reversal for amplitude and area was more pronounced in the experienced group.

members.claim: Our results further support lab checks of the WHM breathing reported by Blades et al. showing increased sympathetic activity as well as findings by Muzik et al. which indicated activity in loci associated with the PAG -insula -pontine-LC region, which aligns with a model in which targeted breathing recruits central arousal systems.
members.doc_name: 6- Hypocapnia Autonomic Signatures and Gamma-Band Reconfiguration During Wim Hof Breathing Method and Cold Immersion

members.claim: IL-10 correlated negatively with pro-inflammatory cytokines.

members.section_heading: Acute Autonomic -Immune Signature of WHM Breathing
